faqs.question: Why are carpenter ants so common in Great Kills?
faqs.answer: Great Kills' coastal location and proximity to Great Kills Park create high humidity levels that carpenter ants thrive in. The neighborhood's many mature trees and older wooden homes provide ideal nesting sites. Moisture from the waterfront areas also attracts these pests to nearby residences.

faqs.question: What's the difference between carpenter ants and regular ants?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are larger (up to 5/8 inch) and cause structural damage by tunneling through wood to build nests, while regular ants are smaller and don't damage wood. Carpenter ants leave behind sawdust-like debris called frass, and you may hear rustling sounds in walls. They require specialized treatment to locate and eliminate the parent colony.
